product_summary.one_sentence_summary: B2B AI literacy workshop for Luxembourg employers that pairs AI Act awareness with practical safe-use training.
product_summary.target_user: HR/L&D directors, SME CEOs and compliance stakeholders in Luxembourg companies that deploy or allow AI tools.
product_summary.core_pain: Teams need enough AI literacy to use AI tools safely and document governance without overpromising compliance.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Improved AI literacy, better internal AI usage and a more defensible training record.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The likely purchase motion is employer-led training, usually run through HR/L&D or people-enablement teams.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: The card frames the workshop around AI Act literacy and audit documentation.
SME leadership may buy directly if the training is positioned as company-wide capability building.

budget_owner.reason: For a workshop format, budget is most likely to sit with HR/L&D or a people development budget.

buyer_influencers.reason: Compliance stakeholders influence the acceptance of AI Act and audit-readiness wording.
Legal review is useful when claims touch regulation, privacy and internal policy language.
SME leadership may influence buy-in if the workshop is sold as a company-wide risk and productivity initiative.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: The AI Act requires providers and deployers of AI systems to ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy among relevant staff and people acting on their behalf.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The Commission states that Article 4 applies to providers and deployers and requires measures to ensure sufficient AI literacy.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: The AI literacy obligation becomes a compliance concern by August 2026.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The obligation already applies from 2 February 2025; supervision and enforcement by national market surveillance authorities start in August 2026. The card compresses that timeline into one deadline.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Luxembourg employer training co-funding via INFPC / Guichet exists and is relevant to training products.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Guichet states that private-sector businesses in Luxembourg can apply for co-funding of continuing vocational training, with exclusions for mandatory regulated-profession training.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Luxinnovation Fit 4 AI / AI support exists as a public AI support route.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Luxinnovation publicly offers Fit 4 AI support. The reviewed public material does not prove that this exact workshop format is directly eligible on the card's terms.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: 14.4% of Luxembourg enterprises used AI technologies in 2023.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Eurostat reports Luxembourg as one of the highest adopters in the EU at 14.4% for 2023.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Only provider combining EU AI Act compliance + hands-on practice + audit documentation.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: House of Training already offers Luxembourg AI training that combines AI Act framing, practical AI use and data-protection/risk content, so the exclusivity claim is too strong.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The AI Literacy Certificate has external compliance significance.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No public source reviewed shows that a provider-issued certificate from this workshop has official regulatory standing.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Audit-ready documentation from this workshop is sufficient for compliance use.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No public source reviewed confirms regulator or authority acceptance of this product's documentation.
No primary public source confirming this exact figure was found in the review.
The reviewed sources do not confirm this amount as it is used in the card's compliance framing.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: SME Package AI 70% (up to €17,500) applies to this workshop.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: Public AI support exists, but the workshop-level eligibility claim is not directly confirmed by the reviewed sources.

regulator_entities.name: European Commission AI Office
regulator_entities.relevance: The Commission publishes AI literacy guidance and supporting materials for Article 4 implementation.

regulator_entities.name: National market surveillance authorities
regulator_entities.relevance: They are the authorities responsible for supervising and enforcing Article 4 from August 2026.

framework_entities.name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act)
framework_entities.relevance: Article 4 creates the AI literacy obligation used by the card's positioning.

framework_entities.name: AI Literacy - Questions & Answers
framework_entities.relevance: Official explanation of the scope, timing and enforcement of Article 4.

framework_entities.name: Repository of AI literacy practices
framework_entities.relevance: Shows that the Commission has already catalogued AI literacy initiatives from providers and deployers.

funding_bodies.name: Luxinnovation Fit 4 AI
funding_bodies.relevance: Relevant public AI support route, though not proven as direct workshop reimbursement.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Providers and deployers of AI systems should take measures to ensure sufficient AI literacy of staff and relevant persons.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: AI providers and deployers in the EU

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Supervision and enforcement of Article 4 begin from August 2026.

subsidies_or_funding.name: Continuing vocational training co-funding
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: Public co-funding exists for company training plans, but it excludes mandatory continuing training for regulated professions.

subsidies_or_funding.name: Fit 4 AI
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: Public AI support exists, but the exact fit for this workshop format was not confirmed.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - Generative artificial intelligences - Fundamentals
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public Luxembourg AI fundamentals course with certificate and productivity orientation.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - How to Navigate the European Union Artificial Intelligence Act
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public AI Act course in Luxembourg that weakens any exclusivity claim.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - AI Driving Licence for Financial Sector Employees (AI Act Compliant)
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Finance-oriented AI literacy product with compliance framing and practical usage content.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The card uses AI Act and audit-readiness language, but it does not show a formal accreditation or recognition route.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: The workshop can be delivered without formal approval, but compliance-oriented content benefits from external SME review.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about AI Act literacy and internal compliance posture are credible with review, but not dependent on formal listing.

proof_stack.reason: For a B2B workshop with substitutes, the first signal is whether employers will buy the format.
A paid pilot would validate willingness to pay and whether the workshop fits internal demand.
The workshop should show improved staff understanding and safer AI usage.

proof_burden.reason: Because the product is sold with compliance-adjacent wording and market substitutes already exist, buyer acceptance plus defensibility proof matters.

dependency_types.reason: External AI governance or legal review would reduce claim-language risk.
Hands-on AI usage and policy guidance should be checked against privacy and data-handling rules.
The workshop is positioned for Luxembourg, so local regulatory and subsidy language matters.
The sales story depends partly on public co-funding, which must be validated carefully.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current scope is Luxembourg corporate training, which is reasonably coherent but still varies by sector and employer maturity.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Scaling across countries would change enforcement context, subsidy logic and buyer expectations.

subsidy_exists.reason: Public co-funding and AI support routes exist in Luxembourg.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The card bundles general training co-funding with AI-specific support and overstates workshop-level certainty.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: The public support routes exist, but the exact workshop-level subsidy promise is not cleanly proven.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The product is not itself regulated, but its claim language sits next to the AI Act and compliance expectations.

claim_language_risk.reason: The card uses compliance, audit-ready and certificate language that is stronger than the public evidence supports for a new workshop.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: The workshop itself does not need formal approval, but compliance claims benefit from external review.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Public Luxembourg substitutes already cover AI literacy, AI Act framing and practical AI usage.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The card relies on dated adoption statistics and uncited upsell assumptions that should not be treated as current market fact.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: Public funding exists, but the card overstates exact workshop-level eligibility and merges different support routes.

positioning_risk.reason: The offer sits between general AI literacy and compliance enablement, which can create expectation mismatch if the workshop stays introductory.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: EU AI Act requires AI literacy from employees.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The legal text is narrower: providers and deployers must take measures to ensure sufficient AI literacy of relevant staff and people acting on their behalf, rather than every employee in every company.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Need to quickly train the team to be compliant by Aug 2026.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The obligation already applies from 2 February 2025, while August 2026 is the start of supervision and enforcement.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Only provider combining EU AI Act compliance + hands-on practice + audit documentation.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: House of Training already offers similar Luxembourg AI courses with AI Act framing and hands-on usage content.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Decide whether AI literacy, audit-ready and compliance wording should be softened before any sales use.
Validate whether the workshop can genuinely be sold under the claimed AI package or only under general company training co-funding.
Rebuild differentiation against House of Training and other public substitutes rather than relying on exclusivity language.

sources.title: First rules of the Artificial Intelligence Act are now applicable
Financial aid for in-company continuing vocational training
8% of EU enterprises used AI technologies in 2023
How to Navigate the European Union Artificial Intelligence Act (EU AI Act)
AI Driving Licence for Financial Sector Employees (AI Act Compliant)
Risk Management - Information Security, Data Protection and Artificial Intelligence
